summary: End-to-end verification of Star Reward Msg 677 §12 (Entry Override + Leave Validity + Month-filter passthrough). Wrote pytest suite for POST /api/star-rewards/entry-override + GET /api/star-rewards/auto/monthly/{id} — 10/10 tests pass (validation, happy paths, repeat-edit no-stack, sysadmin). UI verified end-to-end with Playwright as HR admin: month filter → View History modal opens with correct filtered month expanded (current month collapsed); pencil restricted to leave-related entries only; EntryOverrideDialog exposes all 12 spec fields; submit-disabled logic honours validity + note + differing revised-value; confirmation dialog shows validity + old→new + month name; after submit the override dialog closes, the view-history modal stays open, the filtered month stays expanded, badges (grey 0, VALID LEAVE, Admin adjusted, admin note) render; non-HR user cannot see or open /star-reward.
critical_code_review_comments: Note (not a bug): After the first override, the item.id rendered in ViewHistoryModal for that (date, rule) becomes the override row's id (orphan-override path in server.py ~line 9785). Consequently `edit-entry-<id>`, `validity-badge-<id>`, `admin-note-<id>`, `entry-badge-<id>` all use the override_id after edit. That is functionally correct (testids stay consistent within the same edited entry) but any external automation that captured the pre-edit `edit-entry-<autoId>` must re-query the DOM after submit. Consider surfacing a stable `entry_key` (e.g. `${date}|${rule}`) for automation robustness.

The EntryOverrideDialog outer <Dialog open={!confirmOpen}> pattern (line 1758) hides the parent modal while the confirmation is open. This means selectors inside the parent dialog are non-queryable during confirmation — works fine but can trip up automation that expects both dialogs stackable.

The API allows target_stars == new_stars (compensation=0) as long as validity changes; the FRONTEND blocks that case via the `identical` guard (line 1705). That's intended — the API is more permissive so a validity-only flip is still recordable via other clients.
prior_overrides snapshot (line 9346) stores a bounded 50-item history in each new override row — small but potentially unbounded growth for a hot key. Consider capping the audit-trail depth.
test_credentials: HR: admin / HrAdmin786$ (PINNED). SysAdmin: sysadmin / pass123. Employee: user / pass123. Research employee target: aparna.a (Aparna A, id=b8cf3ffb-01ca-46cf-b33c-42399c3b0fd7).
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_OVR-prefixed override rows against Aparna A for dates in July 2026 and one on 2026-06-15 (Late Sick Notification override, validity=valid, stars -1→0). These prove the flow end-to-end and are visible in the UI. No production data was mutated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Star Reward Msg 677 §12 is FUNCTIONALLY COMPLETE and passes all 12 spec items. Reusable regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_star_reward_override.py — cover POST /api/star-rewards/entry-override + GET /api/star-rewards/auto/monthly/{id}. Frontend UI selectors: `view-history-modal`, `admin-month-<YYYY-MM>`, `edit-entry-<id>`, `entry-override-dialog`, `override-validity-valid|invalid`, `entry-quick--2..+2`, `entry-new-stars`, `entry-note`, `entry-cancel-btn`, `entry-submit-btn`, `entry-confirm-dialog`, `entry-confirm-yes|cancel`, `validity-badge-<id>`, `admin-note-<id>`, `edited-label-<id>`, `entry-badge-<id>`. IMPORTANT: `<id>` used in the testids after an edit is the OVERRIDE row's id (not the auto row id). Non-HR access is correctly denied via role-gated routes.
rca_of_the_issue: No issues found. Verified specifics: (1) filters.month captured in handleViewEmployee (line 175) and applied to setExpandedViewMonth (line 190). (2) isLeaveEntry (line 29) restricts pencil visibility. (3) Backend soft-deletes prior overrides for the same (employee, ref_date, overrides_rule) tuple (line 9340) so repeat edits do NOT stack — confirmed via test_repeat_edit_does_not_accumulate (delta between edits was +1, not +2). (4) Post-submit onApplied (line 916) refreshes monthly view + explicitly restores the expanded month key, so the view-history modal stays open and the filtered month remains expanded.
